content:
# Numbers Chapter 32 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 188–190 | **Source Image:** `NUM_032_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_032_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_032_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0138–pa_0139

---

**1.** And much livestock was to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad, exceedingly mighty; and they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, and behold, the place [was] a place for livestock.

**2.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came and said to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the princes of the congregation, saying,

**3.** "Ataroth and Dibon and Jazer and Nimrah and Heshbon and Elealeh and Sebam and Nebo and Beon —

**4.** the land which YHWH struck before the congregation of Israel — it [is] a land for livestock, and your servants have livestock."

**5.** And they said, "If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants for a possession; do not cause us to cross the Jordan."

**6.** And Moses said to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben, "Shall your brothers go to the war, and you — shall you sit here?

**7.** And why do you discourage the heart of the sons of Israel from crossing over to the land which YHWH has given to them?

**8.** Thus your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land.

**9.** And they went up to the Valley of Eshcol and saw the land, and they discouraged the heart of the sons of Israel so as not to go into the land which YHWH had given to them.

**10.** And the anger of YHWH burned on that day, and he swore, saying,

**11.** 'Surely the men who came up from Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall not see the ground which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, for they have not fully followed after me —

**12.** except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun, for they have fully followed after YHWH.'

**13.** And the anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until [the] finishing of all the generation that had done the evil in the eyes of YHWH.

**14.** And behold, you have risen in the place of your fathers, a brood of sinful men, to add still more upon the burning anger of YHWH toward Israel.

**15.** For if you turn back from after him, he will add still more to leave them in the wilderness, and you will destroy all this people."

**16.** And they drew near to him and said, "Folds for sheep we will build here for our livestock, and cities for our little ones.

**17.** And we ourselves will go armed, hastening, before the sons of Israel, until we have brought them to their place; and our little ones shall dwell in the fortified cities because of the inhabitants of the land.

**18.** We will not return to our houses until the sons of Israel have each inherited his inheritance.

**19.** For we will not inherit with them across the Jordan and beyond, for our inheritance has come to us from the side of the Jordan eastward."

**20.** And Moses said to them, "If you will do this thing — if you will arm yourselves before YHWH for the war,

**21.** and every armed man of you will cross the Jordan before YHWH, until he has driven out his enemies from before him,

**22.** and the land is subdued before YHWH, and afterward you return — then you shall be clear from YHWH and from Israel, and this land shall be to you for a possession before YHWH.

**23.** But if you will not do so, behold, you have sinned against YHWH, and know your sin, which will find you.

**24.** Build for yourselves cities for your little ones and folds for your sheep, and that which has gone out from your mouth you shall do."

**25.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben said to Moses, saying, "Your servants will do as my lord commands.

**26.** Our little ones, our wives, our livestock, and all our cattle shall be there in the cities of Gilead.

**27.** And your servants will cross over, every armed one of the army, before YHWH for the war, as my lord speaks."

**28.** And Moses commanded concerning them Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel.

**29.** And Moses said to them, "If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben cross over with you the Jordan, every [one] armed for the war before YHWH, and the land is subdued before you, then you shall give to them the land of Gilead for a possession.

**30.** But if they do not cross over armed with you, then they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan."

**31.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying, "That which YHWH has spoken to your servants, so we will do.

**32.** We ourselves will cross over armed before YHWH [into] the land of Canaan, and with us [shall be] the possession of our inheritance across the Jordan."

**33.** And Moses gave to them — to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben and to the half-tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph — the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land with its cities in the borders, the cities of the land all around.

**34.** And the sons of Gad built Dibon and Ataroth and Aroer,

**35.** and Atroth-shophan and Jazer and Jogbehah,

**36.** and Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran — fortified cities and folds for sheep.

**37.** And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon and Elealeh and Kiriathaim,

**38.** and Nebo and Baal-meon — [their] names being changed — and Sibmah; and they called by names the names of the cities which they built.

**39.** And the sons of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead and captured it, and he dispossessed the Amorites who [were] in it.

**40.** And Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelt in it.

**41.** And Jair son of Manasseh went and captured their villages, and he called them Havvoth-jair.

**42.** And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its daughters, and he called it Nobah, by his name.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 1 — "exceedingly mighty" (עָצוּם מְאֹד):** The Hebrew עָצוּם literally means "mighty/numerous." This describes the vastness of the livestock holdings of Reuben and Gad. The LC text is clear; Vaticanus (LXX) renders this with πολλὰ σφόδρα ("very many"), interpreting עָצוּם in its quantitative sense.

2. **Verse 3 — Place names:** The LC lists nine place names: Ataroth (עֲטָרוֹת), Dibon (דִּיבֹן), Jazer (יַעְזֵר), Nimrah (נִמְרָה), Heshbon (חֶשְׁבּוֹן), Elealeh (אֶלְעָלֵה), Sebam (שְׂבָם), Nebo (נְבוֹ), and Beon (בְּעוֹן). Vaticanus has slight orthographic variations but the same list. "Beon" in v.3 corresponds to "Baal-meon" in v.38, the shortened form likely avoiding the theophoric element "Baal."

3. **Verse 7 — "discourage" (תְנִיאוּן):** From the root נוא in the Hiphil, meaning "to hinder, discourage, dissuade." This same verb appears in v.9. The LXX renders with διαστρέφετε ("you turn aside/pervert"), interpreting the discouragement as a distortion of will.

4. **Verse 11 — Oath formula:** The Hebrew אִם־יִרְאוּ is a negative oath formula — literally "if they shall see" meaning "they shall surely not see." This is an elliptical oath where the apodosis is suppressed. Translated here as "shall not see" to preserve the intended meaning.

5. **Verse 12 — "fully followed" (מִלְאוּ אַחֲרֵי):** Literally "they filled after" YHWH — an idiom meaning complete, wholehearted devotion. Caleb is identified as הַקְּנִזִּי (the Kenizzite), indicating his clan affiliation within Judah.

6. **Verse 14 — "brood" (תַּרְבּוּת):** From רבה ("to increase"), this word means "increase, offspring, brood." It carries a pejorative sense here — "a brood of sinful men." The LXX renders with σύστασις/σύστρεμμα ("gathering/band"), a less biologically-charged term.

7. **Verse 17 — "hastening" (חֻשִׁים):** The LC reads חֻשִׁים, meaning "hastening, hurrying." Some manuscripts and the Samaritan Pentateuch read חֲלוּצִים ("armed/equipped"), which appears in some ancient versions. The LXX has πρόφυλαξ ("advance guard"), which may reflect a different Vorlage. EMET follows the LC reading.

8. **Verse 24 — "that which has gone out from your mouth":** The Hebrew וְהַיֹּצֵא מִפִּיכֶם תַּעֲשׂוּ is a solemn charge — what they have verbally committed, they must perform. This reflects the binding nature of vows in Israelite law (cf. Numbers 30).

9. **Verse 33 — Half-tribe of Manasseh:** The sudden introduction of the half-tribe of Manasseh (חֲצִי שֵׁבֶט מְנַשֶּׁה) here is notable, as they were not part of the earlier negotiations (vv.1-32). This has generated significant scholarly discussion. The LC text is unambiguous; Vaticanus likewise includes Manasseh. The text presents Moses' allocation as a sovereign act extending beyond the original petitioners.

10. **Verse 38 — "names being changed" (מוּסַבֹּת שֵׁם):** The phrase מוּסַבֹּת שֵׁם (Hophal participle of סבב) indicates the names were "turned around" or changed — likely because the original names contained the theophoric element "Baal" (as in Baal-meon) which was offensive. The LC preserves this editorial gloss within the text itself.

11. **Verse 39 — Singular/plural shift:** "The sons of Machir... went (וַיֵּלְכוּ, plural)... and he dispossessed (וַיּוֹרֶשׁ, singular)." The shift from plural to singular is preserved as it stands in the LC, reflecting either a collective singular usage or the prominence of Machir as clan head.

12. **Verse 41 — "Havvoth-jair":** The Hebrew חַוֹּת יָאִיר means literally "the villages/tent-camps of Jair." The word חַוָּה refers to a small, unwalled settlement or tent-village, characteristic of the semi-nomadic settlements in Gilead.

13. **Verse 42 — "daughters" (בְּנֹתֶיהָ):** Referring to satellite towns or dependent villages surrounding Kenath. This is a standard Hebrew idiom for subsidiary settlements.

14. **LC vs. Vaticanus — General agreement:** Numbers 32 shows strong agreement between the Masoretic tradition (LC) and the LXX (Vaticanus), with no major textual divergences. The Vaticanus column arrangement on pa_0138-0139 preserves the full chapter in uncial script with the expected Greek renderings of Hebrew place names and idioms.
